rows.evidence_statement: UniProt identifies KcsA as a pH-gated potassium channel from Streptomyces lividans and places it in the potassium channel family.
rows.grammar_role: KcsA target identity and potassium-channel family class.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_date_or_version: UniProtKB reviewed P0A334; last annotation update 2026-01-28; accessed 2026-06-11
rows.source_name: UniProtKB P0A334 pH-gated potassium channel KcsA

rows.evidence_statement: UniProt function annotation reports pH-gated potassium-channel activity and monovalent cation preference K(+) > Rb(+) > NH4(+) >> Na(+) > Li(+).
rows.grammar_role: K+ selectivity grammar; not a coordinate-derived ion-contact table.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_date_or_version: UniProtKB reviewed P0A334; last annotation update 2026-01-28; accessed 2026-06-11

rows.evidence_statement: UniProt annotates KcsA as a cell-membrane multi-pass membrane protein with transmembrane helices and notes residues 62-79 are situated in the membrane and important for channel structure/properties.
rows.grammar_role: Membrane/transmembrane operator context before any native-coordinate selection.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_date_or_version: UniProtKB reviewed P0A334; last annotation update 2026-01-28; accessed 2026-06-11

rows.evidence_statement: The KcsA sequence contains the canonical potassium-channel TVGYG filter signature, used here only as sequence/family grammar and not as a coordinate-contact constraint.
rows.grammar_role: Filter/signature grammar for K+-channel pore context.
rows.provenance_notes: External annotation/literature/state evidence only; not a PDB contact table, not an internal runtime report, not native-metric selection, no MD.
rows.source_name: KcsA canonical potassium-channel TVGYG selectivity-filter signature
rows.source_url_or_citation: UniProtKB P0A334 canonical sequence; Heginbotham L et al. Biophys J. 1994;66:1061-1067; PubMed:8038378
